label: Localized cleaning product irritant rash without burn or anaphylaxis
summary: Your symptoms fit a localized irritant skin reaction from a cleaning product.
found: Your clinician diagnosed a localized cleaning product irritant skin reaction. We did not find chemical burn, anaphylaxis, mucosal involvement, skin infection, or another rash emergency today.
home: Avoid the suspected trigger if it is known.
Wash gently with mild soap and water, and avoid scrubbing the rash.
Use cool compresses, moisturizers, or coverings only as your clinician recommended.
meds: Use creams, ointments, itch medicine, or allergy medicine only if prescribed or recommended.
Do not use leftover steroid, antibiotic, or allergy medicine unless your clinician told you to.
return: Trouble breathing, throat tightness, swelling of the lips or tongue, fainting, or a bodywide reaction.
Burning pain, blistering, skin peeling, mouth sores, eye symptoms, fever, spreading redness, pus, red streaks, or rapidly worsening rash.
Symptoms that worsen or do not improve as expected.
follow: Follow up with primary care, urgent care, dermatology, allergy, poison control as directed, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of localized cleaning product irritant skin reaction.
No chemical burn, anaphylaxis, systemic allergic reaction, mucosal involvement, eye involvement, skin infection, severe medication reaction, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion: Chemical burn, eye exposure, anaphylaxis, airway symptoms, hypotension, systemic allergic reaction, mucosal lesions, severe cutaneous adverse reaction, skin infection, rapidly progressive rash, immunocompromised host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed dermatology/allergy/toxicology plan.
must_not_miss: Cellulitis or severe cutaneous adverse reaction.

terms: clinician diagnosis localized cleaning product irritant rash
discharge diagnosis localized cleaning product irritant rash
localized cleaning product irritant rash diagnosed

high_confidence_terms: clinician diagnosis localized cleaning product irritant rash
localized cleaning product irritant rash diagnosed
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus supports contact dermatitis framing from irritant exposures, trigger avoidance, gentle skin care, clinician-directed treatment, and escalation for severe allergic, chemical, ocular, infectious, or mucosal red flags. This expansion is limited to clinician-diagnosed localized cleaning product irritant skin reaction with chemical burn, anaphylaxis, infection, eye, mucosal, pregnancy/pediatric, severe-rash, and specialist-directed pathways absent.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis of localized cleaning product irritant skin reaction
no infection, mucosal, or severe-rash red flags
